description_long: This is a medium-sized crab with a robust, dark brown to grayish exoskeleton and a broad, slightly flattened carapace. Its two prominent claws (chelae) are asymmetrical, with one noticeably larger than the other, and are a lighter, almost whitish color compared to the rest of the body. The crab has eight walking legs, each jointed and covered in a similar dark hue, with some lighter segments near the tips. Its eyes are set on short stalks protruding from the front of the carapace, providing a wide field of vision. The ventral side and the joints of the legs show subtle orange and cream tones, adding to the natural coloration. The overall appearance is typical of a terrestrial or semi-terrestrial crab that might be found in urban parks, near water features, or in landscaped environments.
description: A robust, dark brown crab with two prominent claws and eight jointed legs, featuring eyes on short stalks and subtle orange and cream highlights.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the broad carapace, both claws (one larger), and eyes on stalks clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the larger claw raised, the segmentation of the legs, and the lighter underside.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace, all eight legs splayed, and the symmetry of the body.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view focusing on the smaller claw and the curvature of the legs.
